title: Disk is at 95%, fix it before it kills the server
problem: You get a alert at 2am — disk is at 95%. The server will stop accepting writes if it hits 100% and the app will crash. Find what is eating the disk, clean it up and get usage back under 70%.

setup.description: Create fake large files to simulate a disk space problem.
setup.note: This creates 450MB of fake log files. Safe to delete entirely after the scenario.

hints: Start broad then narrow down — df -h shows disk usage by partition, du -h narrows it to directories
du -sh /* 2>/dev/null will show you the biggest directories at root level — follow the largest one down
Large log files are the most common culprit — find / -name '*.log' -size +50M 2>/dev/null to hunt them down
solution:
1. Check overall disk:       df -h  # shows all partitions and usage percentage
2. Find biggest dirs:        du -sh ~/* 2>/dev/null | sort -rh  # sort by size, biggest first
3. Drill into disklab:       du -sh ~/disklab/logs/app/*  # see which log files are largest
4. Hunt large log files:     find ~/disklab -name '*.log' -size +50M  # find logs over 50MB
5. Delete old logs:          rm ~/disklab/logs/app/debug.log  # remove the biggest offenders
6. Truncate live log:        truncate -s 0 ~/disklab/logs/app/app.log  # empty without deleting, safe for active logs
7. Confirm cleanup:          df -h  # verify usage has dropped
